source: ExchangeRate-API (open.er-api.com) mid-market rates 22:30 UTC, cross-referenced with Wise and Trading Economics
baseNote: Rates show ZMW per 1 unit of foreign currency. ↑ ZMW = Kwacha weakening (depreciating).
mpRateNote: Monetary Policy Rate held at 13.50% — reduced 75bps from 14.25% on Feb 11, 2026. BOZ held rate at May 2026 review. Next review: August 2026.

USD.note: XE Jun 25: 1 USD = 18.040 ZMW (1 ZMW = 0.0554 USD). Kwacha weakened further vs USD from 17.889 (Jun 23) — 0.8% depreciation this week as USD strengthened globally.

GBP.note: 1 GBP = 23.765 ZMW (XE Jun 25). Sterling firmer vs kwacha from 23.641 (Jun 23).

EUR.note: 1 EUR = 20.495 ZMW (XE Jun 25). Euro near flat vs kwacha from 20.492 (Jun 23).

ZAR.note: 1 ZAR = 1.088 ZMW (XE Jun 25). Rand essentially flat vs kwacha from 1.089 (Jun 23). 1 ZMW = 0.919 ZAR.

BWP.note: Botswana pula. ExchangeRate-API cross: 1 ZMW = 0.787 BWP. No significant change.

CNY.note: Chinese yuan. ExchangeRate-API: 1 ZMW = 0.379 CNY → 1 CNY = 2.638 ZMW. Kwacha weakening slightly against yuan.

trendAnalysis.direction: Kwacha continued weakening this week — USD/ZMW broke above 18.00 for the first time since mid-June. USD strength globally pushing kwacha 0.8% softer since Jun 23.
drivers: Record copper output 890K tonnes (2025) — strong export earnings
Record maize harvest >4M tonnes — reduced food import needs
Debt restructuring 94% complete — sovereign risk premium collapsing
IMF successor programme talks scheduled July 2026 — staff visit confirmed
MPR at 13.5% — high real yields attracting carry trade inflows
Yuan mining tax corridor reducing USD demand
Gross international reserves at historic high of US$6.5 billion
trendAnalysis.outlook: Kwacha testing 18.00 resistance after breaking through on USD strength. Market watching BOZ for any intervention signals. Range shifting to 17.80–18.20. Copper prices and IMF programme progress remain key catalysts. BOZ likely to lean against excessive weakness.
trendAnalysis.projection3m: USD/ZMW 16.50–18.20 range. Expect continued appreciation bias but increased volatility ahead of elections.
trendAnalysis.projection6m: USD/ZMW 16.00–19.00 range. Post-election clarity determines direction.
